mnemonics: PEMDAS Priority: Like a priority encoder gives the highest number its output, PEMDAS gives the highest priority operation its result first.
keyPoints: An encoder converts 2^n inputs into an n-bit binary code.
A priority encoder resolves multiple active inputs by outputting the code for the highest-priority one.
The output of an encoder is the binary equivalent of the active input line.
commonMistakes: Assuming an encoder can have multiple active inputs without a priority scheme, leading to invalid outputs.
Forgetting to handle the case where no inputs are active, often requiring an additional 'valid' output bit.
Misidentifying the priority order (MSB vs LSB) in a custom priority encoder.
